chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P No. D-1877 of 2024 [Mst. Irshad Bibi v. Province of Sindh & another] Before: Mr. Justice Arbab Ali Hakro Mr. Justice Riazat Ali Sahar Mr. Muntazir Mahdi advocate for petitioner. Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto Additional Advocate General, Sindh. Mr. Amjad Ali advocate for respondents No.2 to 5. Mr. Muhammad Yousif Khan appearing on behalf of TMC Latifabad. Mr. Farhan Khan Dayo Senior Accounts Officer H.M.C. Date of hearing & Order: - 12.03.2026. O R D E R RIAZAT ALI SAHAR, J: - Through this Constitutional Petition filed under Article 199 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, the petitioner is seeking following reliefs:- A) That, this Honourable Court may be pleased to direct the respondent No.1 to 5 issue/pay the pension, gratuity and other back benefits if any up till now the petitioner forthwith. B) Costs of the petition may be saddled upon the respondents. C) Any other relief(s) which this Honourable Court deems fit, just and proper in favour of the petitioner may be granted. 2 2. During course of arguments, it is agreed by the counsel for the parties that; (a) The monthly pension would be provided by successor T.M.C. Shah Latifabad and other dues including commutation, gratuity etc. would be paid by H.M.C. Hyderabad. (b) The T.M.A. Shah Latifabad will send case of the petitioner within a week’s time. (c) It is settled that the due amount would be paid to the petitioner within two months i.e. release of pension along with arrears immediately in three installments by way of post-paid cheques within three months. (d) In case of failure, contempt proceedings shall be initiated against the delinquent officer(s). 3.

Hyderabad. (b) The T.M.A. Shah Latifabad will send case of the petitioner within a week’s time. (c) It is settled that the due amount would be paid to the petitioner within two months i.e. release of pension along with arrears immediately in three installments by way of post-paid cheques within three months. (d) In case of failure, contempt proceedings shall be initiated against the delinquent officer(s). 3. In view of above settlement, instant petition stands disposed of with direction to the concerned competent authorities to ensure that the entire amount is finalized and released expeditiously within the stipulated time in accordance with law. JUDGE JUDGE *Abdullah Channa/PS*
